message:
[LOW] fix(sync): clean up manifest temp file on write/fsync failure

write_manifest_atomically only removed the temp file if durable_rename
failed. If write_all or sync_all errored — ENOSPC, EIO, EINTR on a slow
fsync — the temp file was left behind. The PID-derived suffix means a
later invocation with the same process id (e.g., after pid wraparound on
a long-running container) could hit File::create with O_EXCL semantics
and abort with a stale-temp error instead of continuing.

Extract the cleanup closure and inspect_err each of write_all / sync_all
/ durable_rename so every failure path removes the temp file before
returning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
